text:
Market today: The mood is tense, driven by escalating geopolitical risk from Iran/Hormuz and surging oil prices. This could trigger a broader risk-off move, potentially hurting your tech/chip names due to supply chain concerns and general market fear.

Your holdings: SOXL (-24.46%) and SPAL (-27.71%) are screaming for attention. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning they decay and amplify drops, which is extra painful on margin. Their "SELL" and "NEW" statuses, coupled with significant losses, make them critical.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further escalation in the Middle East and how the broader market reacts. Also, monitor the chip sector's resilience given the mixed news.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged positions in a volatile geopolitical environment; new buys are secondary.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming SOXL given its -24.46% loss and "SELL" status. Respect the stop at 200.
SPAL: Review SPAL, a new leveraged holding down -27.71%. Adhere strictly to its stop at 21.38.
No new buys today - hold and watch.
